issues.title: Response parameter naming: "resource" (array) vs "resource"/"resources" (string/array)
issues.author: panva
issues.body:
Should the response use `"resource"` (a JSON string) when a single resource is indicated and `"resources"` (a JSON array of strings) when multiple are indicated? Or should a single parameter name be used with consistently one type?

Using a single name (`"resource"`) with a JSON array value aligns with RFC 8707's request parameter naming but introduces a type difference (repeated query/form parameter vs. JSON array). Using two names would make the cardinality explicit but diverges from how `scope` works.
